name: set
description: The standalone lodash.set package as a vendorable file. Useful when you need lodash-style deep path assignment without pulling the package into your dependency graph.
tagline: Write deep values into dynamic paths.
summary: This page ships the published lodash.set file in both raw and normalized forms. The value is to preserve a known deep-assignment behavior locally instead of re-implementing it ad hoc.
nativeAlternative: Direct assignment works for shallow cases, but dynamic string paths and intermediate object creation make lodash-style set semantics harder to replace safely.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization for modern TS projects. The goal is easier import and build compatibility, not changed behavior.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Published package file kept as-is for older teams or repos that still prefer the original CommonJS distribution.

note: Raw and normalized views are both available. Use normalized when you want an ESM and TypeScript-friendly file, and raw when you want the original published package shape.
